note: Main treatment in the paper. Explicit country-level events define the cohort, which is then replicated to all in-sample cities within treated countries. Regional rows under the same thresholds are retained as corroborative support rather than as separate within-country rollout. The pipeline design summary records 721 direct-core rows; the persisted enriched registry reproduces 720 explicit cohort-defining rows plus 119 regional support rows under the same thresholds.
Captures mobility, transport, and urban-services interventions under the same country-year common-shock rollout rule.
Digital connectivity and innovation/industry events; city treatment still inherits the country-year cohort rather than a city-specific rollout date.
Institutional reform, energy transition, and climate-resilience events; anticipation patterns should be read against country-year timing rather than city-specific adoption.
